title: Substrate map — adopter axis→substrate bindings (brownfield)
description: Formal shape of substrate-map.yaml — the OPTIONAL adopter-side file by which a brownfield project binds each methodology axis (type, priority, workstream, state) onto whatever its immutable tracker already encodes (per project-management:DEC-036, read-path contract ADR-026). The file's presence is the activation signal: ABSENT ⇒ greenfield (every axis reads the kit's own labels, byte-unchanged); PRESENT ⇒ each axis resolves through its declared binding, and any axis NOT listed degrades (treated as `unsupported`, NOT greenfield). Per axis EXACTLY ONE binding: `label` (a value→value remap onto existing labels), `title-prefix` (a value→prefix remap), `derive` (a detection predicate, lifecycle-state), or `unsupported: true`. Document-shaped schema: the file IS the resource (no id collection). Companion per COR-018 / the .pkit/schemas/ area conventions.

pkit_schema.description: Self-describing adopter-data binding (per COR-023). Expected value: 'project-management:substrate-map'.

binds_to.description: Per-schema fallback binding (per COR-023). Present on the capability-side schema YAML so the resolver can match an adopter substrate-map.yaml file without an explicit `pkit_schema:` field.

axes.description: Per-axis bindings. A key MAY be omitted entirely — an omitted axis in a present map is treated as `unsupported`, NOT greenfield (DEC-036 / ADR-026 the load-bearing rule). Only the four methodology axes are recognised; unknown keys are rejected.

type.description: How the `type` axis is encoded in this tracker. The Feature-in-Feature containment invariant is keyed on `type` and stays HARD regardless of this binding (DEC-036 D4).

priority.description: How the `priority` axis is encoded (e.g. native P0/P1/P2 labels via a `label` remap).

workstream.description: How the `workstream` axis is encoded, or `unsupported: true` when the tracker has no workstream encoding.

state.description: How the lifecycle-state axis is encoded. Typically a `derive` predicate over open/closed + a blocked label (the DEC-033 detector swap), not a label remap.

axis_binding.description: Exactly one binding per axis: `label`, `title-prefix`, `derive`, or `unsupported: true`. An optional `default` (a substrate value to seed on writes) MAY accompany any of them.

default.description: Optional substrate value to seed on a write when the caller supplies none (e.g. `P1` for a priority axis bound to native priority labels). A read-side note for the writer; the read seam never invents it.

unsupported.description: Mark the axis explicitly unsupported — the feature needing it degrades (advisory/disabled). Equivalent to omitting the axis from a present map; written explicitly when the adopter wants the intent on record.

label_binding.description: Bind the axis to an existing label set via a kit-value→adopter-label remap. The resolved substrate is the adopter's OWN label string (e.g. `P0`), never the kit's `<axis>:<value>` form.

remap.description: Map from a methodology value (the kit's own, e.g. `High`) to the adopter's existing label name (e.g. `P0`). A methodology value with NO entry here is value-unresolvable within this bound axis — it degrades only the rules depending on that value, never the axis as a whole (ADR-026 fourth arm).

title_prefix_binding.description: Bind the axis to a title-prefix convention via a kit-value→prefix remap (e.g. `task → [Task]`). The resolved substrate is the prefix string the adopter already uses in issue titles.

remap.description: Map from a methodology value to the adopter's title-prefix string. A methodology value with no entry is value-unresolvable (the AUJ `[Task]`/`[Epic]`-but-no-`[Feature]` case, ADR-026 fourth arm).

derive_binding.description: Bind the axis to a detection predicate over tracker reality — the DEC-033 detector swap for lifecycle-state (open/closed + a blocked label) in place of `state:*` labels. The predicate ENGINE (first-matching-detection-wins, reduced state set) lands with the detector work; this schema declares the binding shape only.

from.description: The tracker signal the predicate reads (e.g. `open-closed`). Forward-declared; the detector engine that consumes it is sibling Wave-2 work.

states.description: Map from a derived (collapsed) state id to the condition that detects it. The condition grammar is the detector engine's concern; declared here as a string per state so the binding is well-formed ahead of the engine.
